task_completion.description: Create at least four new partition walls and define four rooms with descriptive names.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Sweet Home 3D 2D plan, look for at least four drawn wall segments dividing the space. There should be four distinct room areas created using the 'Create rooms' tool, labeled with names like 'Dirty Treatment Area', 'Clean Inpainting Area', 'Quarantine/Art Storage', and 'Photography Documentation'.

task_completion.description: Apply distinct floor colors or textures to at least two rooms.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan or 3D view, observe the floor areas of the defined rooms. At least two of the rooms must have different floor colors or textures applied compared to the default or each other.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Dirty Treatment Area with required items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Locate the 'Dirty Treatment Area' in the plan or 3D view. Verify there are at least two sinks and two large workbenches placed within this specific zone.

task_completion.description: Equip the Clean Inpainting Area with required items.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Locate the 'Clean Inpainting Area' in the plan or 3D view. Verify there are at least three desks, three chairs, and four lamps placed within this specific zone.

task_completion.description: Enclose the Quarantine/Storage room with a door and furnish it.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Locate the 'Quarantine/Art Storage' room. Verify it is enclosed by walls with at least one door placed in the wall. Inside the room, verify there are at least five shelving units or storage cabinets.

task_completion.description: Generate a 3D photo render and save it to the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file named 'conservation_lab_render.png' on the Desktop or an image viewer showing the rendered 3D view of the lab. The Sweet Home 3D window title should also indicate the project is saved (no asterisk next to the filename).

integrity.description: The agent used the Sweet Home 3D GUI to build the layout.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Sweet Home 3D application open with the 2D plan and 3D view actively being modified, rather than just terminal windows.

integrity.description: The generated render is a genuine output from Sweet Home 3D.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'conservation_lab_render.png' image should visually match the layout designed in the Sweet Home 3D workspace, showing the correct walls, floors, and furniture.

integrity.description: The agent did not create dummy files to bypass rendering.
integrity.visual_evidence: The render file should be a valid image file containing the 3D scene, not an empty file or a text file renamed to .png created via terminal commands.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires a passing score of 70/100. The starter file is 'conservation_lab_starter.sh3d'. The expected render must be saved exactly at '/home/ga/Desktop/conservation_lab_render.png'. Verify the specific furniture counts in each named room: Dirty Treatment (>=2 sinks, >=2 workbenches), Clean Inpainting (>=3 desks, >=3 chairs, >=4 lamps), Quarantine (>=1 door, >=5 shelving/cabinets).
